libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property is indicated. Tests should target the 2.0 API: RenderingContext and SkinResourceLoader are gone; use DocumentRenderingContext and account for DefaultSiteRenderer's injected Doxia/Velocity/Plexus collaborators, preferably via Sisu/Plexus-style setup or controlled test injection. The xhtml5 parser module is already transitive; add parser modules in test code only if deliberately rendering APT/XDoc/FML inputs.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 has public APIs and runtime code referencing org.apache.maven.artifact classes, while the artifact marks maven-artifact as provided; outside Maven's own runtime the TCK test classpath must supply it.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 1.9.1 tests rely on removed 2.0.0 APIs, so compile failures are expected unless tests are rewritten.
Rendering common Maven site formats such as APT or XDoc may require explicit Doxia parser module dependencies if the generated test chooses those formats; this is not required for xhtml5 coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 declares org.apache.maven:maven-artifact as a provided dependency, but its public/runtime classes require it; the plain Gradle test scaffold does not provide it, so meaningful tests can fail before exercising the renderer.
